sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 19, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves active green frame right by 3 cols VERIFIED', 'LEFT': 'Moves active green frame left by 3 cols VERIFIED', 'UP': 'Moves active green frame up by 3 rows VERIFIED', 'DOWN': 'Moves active green frame down by 3 rows VERIFIED', 'MOUSE': 'Clicking on green frame selects it (gives white center), clicking on center pixel changes its color VERIFIED'}
- Object taxonomy: {'g': 'gray background', 'w': 'white panels/squares', 'p': 'purple vertical bar (barrier between panels)', 'N': 'light green 3x3 frames (movable tokens)', 'c': 'charcoal 5x5 frames (targets)', 'W': 'white single pixel (selected token center)', 'B': 'black single pixel (unselected token center)'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'row 63, charcoal strip'}
- Win pattern: {'rule': 'Move each green frame into a charcoal target frame. Select frame with MOUSE, then use arrow keys to move it.'}
Current level:
- World model: Right frame active (white center). Currently at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. Just executed UP+6xRIGHT. Need to check if frame reached right target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. Left frame at rows 30-32, cols 18-20 with charcoal center (not active). Left target at rows 32-36, cols 11-15.
- Goal model: Move green frames into charcoal target frames. Right frame needs to go to right target (rows 26-30, cols 44-48). Left frame needs to go to left target (rows 32-36, cols 11-15).
- Recent findings: ['MOUSE selects active frame (gives white center)', 'Arrow keys move active frame by 3 pixels', 'RIGHT frame moved right with RIGHT key', 'UP moves frame up by 3 rows', 'Batch of UP+6xRIGHT just executed - need to verify result']
- Open questions: ['Can frames pass through purple barrier?', 'Does the frame need to be centered in the target?', 'What happens when frame reaches target?']
- Plan: Check if right frame reached target. If yes, select left frame and move it to left target. If not, adjust movement.
- Strategy log: ['Initial exploration: tested RIGHT/LEFT/UP/DOWN on left frame (steps 1-6). Discovered MOUSE selection mechanic (step 7). Moved right frame toward target (steps 8-10).']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 18 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the ascii output, I can see:
- Row 30: wwwwwNNNppppppwwwwwcccccw
- Row 31: wwwwwNWNppppppwwwwwwwwwww
- Row 32: wwwwwNNNppppppwwwwwwwwwww
The right green frame is at cols 30-32 (NNN/NWN/NNN), and the purple barrier is at cols 33-38 (pppppp). The frame is stuck against the barrier.
Looking at the layout:
- Left white panel: rows 26-33 (approximately), cols ~10-29
- Purple barrier: rows 21-39, cols 33-38 (but varies slightly)
- Right white panel: rows 26-33, cols 39+
- Right target: rows 26-30, cols 44-48
The frame at rows 30-32 can't go RIGHT (blocked by barrier at cols 33-38) and can't go UP (blocked by panel boundary at row 26).
Wait, let me look more carefully. The white panel on the left seems to be at rows 26-33. The frame at rows 30-32 is within this panel. If I try UP, it would go to rows 27-29, which should still be within the panel. But earlier UP didn't work.
Actually, let me re-examine. In the ascii:
- Row 26: ggggggggppppppwwwwwcccccw - no white panel on the left side
- Row 30: wwwwwNNNppppppwwwwwcccccw - white panel on left
So the left white panel starts at some row between 26 and 30. Let me check rows 26-29 more carefully.
Looking at rows 26-29, cols 25-32:
- Row 26: gggggggg (all gray)
- Row 27: gggggggg (all gray)
- Row 28: gggggggg (all gray)
- Row 29: gggggggg (all gray)
- Row 30: wwwwwNNN (white panel + frame)
So the left white panel is at rows 30-32 only! It's a 3-row high panel. The frame is at rows 30-32, which is exactly the panel height. This means the frame can't go UP or DOWN - it would leave the panel.
But wait, the frame is 3x3, and the panel is also 3 rows high. So the frame is perfectly fitted vertically. The frame can only move LEFT or RIGHT within the panel.
Now, the panel extends from cols ~25 to ~29 (wwwww is 5 chars, then NNN is 3 chars). The frame at cols 30-32 is at the right edge of the panel, right next to the barrier.
So the right green frame is trapped in the left panel! It can't cross the barrier.
This makes me think the goal might be different. Maybe:
1. Both green frames need to go to the left target (but that doesn't make sense with two frames)
2. The right green frame needs to go to the left target, and the left green frame needs to stay or go somewhere else
3. There's a different mechanic I'm missing
Let me look at the left target position: rows 32-36, cols 11-15. The left green frame is at rows 30-32, cols 18-20. To get the left frame to the left target, it needs to go LEFT and DOWN.
But the right frame is at rows 30-32, cols 30-32. To get it to the left target at rows 32-36, cols 11-15, it would need to go LEFT many times and DOWN once. But the left target is only 5x5, and the frame is 3x3, so they can overlap.
Wait, maybe the right green frame should go to the left target! Let me try moving it LEFT and see if it can reach the left target.
Actually, let me reconsider. Looking at the layout:
- Left target: rows 32-36, cols 11-15 (5x5 charcoal frame)
- Left green frame: rows 30-32, cols 18-20 (3x3)
- Right green frame: rows 30-32, cols 30-32 (3x3)
The left target is at rows 32-36, which is below the left panel (rows 30-32). So to reach the left target, a frame would need to go DOWN, which might not be possible if the panel boundary blocks it.
Hmm, but the left target is a charcoal frame on the gray background, not inside the white panel. So maybe frames can move outside the white panel?
Let me try moving the right frame LEFT to see if it can reach the left target area. But first, let me check if the frame can move outside the white panel by trying LEFT.
Actually, I realize I should focus on what's actionable. The right frame is active (white center). Let me try moving it LEFT to see if it can reach the left target. The left target is at cols 11-15, and the frame is at cols 30-32. That's 15-18 columns away, which is 5-6 LEFT moves.
But wait, the left green frame is at cols 18-20, which is between the right frame and the left target. So the right frame would need to pass through or around the left frame.
Let me try a different approach: select the left frame and move it to the left target first. Then see if the right frame can also go there or somewhere else.
Actually, I just realized something. The left target is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. The left panel is at rows 30-33 (approximately). So the target extends below the panel. Maybe frames can move outside the panel?
Let me try: select left frame, then move it LEFT and DOWN toward the left target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The right frame is trapped. Let me try selecting the left frame and moving it to the left target.
# Left frame is at rows 30-32, cols 18-20. Left target is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15.
# To select left frame, click on its center (row 31, col 19)

# First, let me verify the left frame position and select it
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
n_frames = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
for n in n_frames:
    b = n['boundary']
    center_r = sum(p[0] for p in b) // 4
    center_c = sum(p[1] for p in b) // 4
    print(f"N frame: boundary={b}, center=({center_r},{center_c})")

# Select the left frame by clicking its center
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 19}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves active green frame right by 3 cols VERIFIED", "LEFT": "Moves active green frame left by 3 cols VERIFIED", "UP": "Moves active green frame up by 3 rows VERIFIED", "DOWN": "Moves active green frame down by 3 rows VERIFIED", "MOUSE": "Clicking on green frame selects it (gives white center) VERIFIED"}, "object_taxonomy": {"g": "gray background", "w": "white panels/squares", "p": "purple vertical bar", "N": "light green 3x3 frames (movable tokens)", "c": "charcoal 5x5 frames (targets)", "W": "white single pixel (selected token center)", "B": "black single pixel (unselected token center)"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "row 63, charcoal strip"}, "win_pattern": {"rule": "Move each green frame into a charcoal target frame"}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Right frame trapped at rows 30-32, cols 30-32 by purple barrier. Left frame at rows 30-32, cols 18-20. Left target at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. Right target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48.", "goal_model": "Move green frames into charcoal target frames - need to figure out which frame goes where", "recent_findings": ["Right frame cannot cross purple barrier", "Left white panel is only 3 rows high (rows 30-32)"], "open_questions": ["Can frames move outside white panels?", "Which frame goes to which target?"], "current_plan": "Select left frame, try to move it to left target", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["UP on right frame (blocked)", "RIGHT on right frame (blocked by barrier)"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
